name: PKS Design via RetroTide
description: Given a target polyketide SMILES, proposes chimeric type-I modular PKS designs that can synthesise the compound's carbon backbone without post-PKS tailoring enzymes, ranked by chemical similarity to the target.

inputs.name: smiles
inputs.description: SMILES string of the target polyketide molecule. Must be parseable by RDKit. Example: "CCCC(=O)O" for butyric acid.

inputs.name: max_designs
inputs.description: Maximum number of PKS designs to return per round, ordered best-first by chemical similarity (1–25, default 5).

inputs.name: similarity
inputs.description: Similarity metric used to rank designs against the target. Accepted values: "atompairs" (default) or "atomatompath".

outputs.description: Ordered list of PKS design objects (best similarity first). Each object contains: rank (integer), similarity (number, 0–1), product_smiles (string), exact_match (boolean), and modules (array of module descriptor objects). Each module descriptor contains: starter (boolean), domains (array of strings), extender (string or null), at_specificity (string or null).

examples.notes: Illustrative output; exact values depend on RetroTide's current domain database.
3-hydroxybutyric acid; RetroTide should find a KR-containing design.
